notes: Attempted to run the game directly from Steam with Steam Play. Tried Default, and by checking the "Use this tool instead of..." with 3.7-8, 3.7-8 beta and 3.16 beta. None of these options worked. Default runs the intro video until it locks up at random times. If I use Default and hit esc immediately sometimes I can get to the menu but then it locks up when any menu option is chosen. All other versions than Default lock up at either the Microsoft Logo or with a blank black screen.
title: Sunset Overdrive

notes: Launches without tweaks but often hangs while trying to load assets forcing me to kill the process. Disabling Esync improves stability somewhat, though it doesn't fix the issue completely. There are very obvious graphical glitches in the form of dark, boxy splotches on the screen, but otherwise the in-game performance is reasonably good. Full controller support.
title: Sunset Overdrive

notes: Graphical glitches galore, and stability is bad, I'm not getting any sound and performance is exceptionally poor, BUT it does run, so there is SOME promise for this title.
title: Sunset Overdrive

notes: Disable Esync to fix crashing at the intro screen. During gameplay, the music seems to cut out, but I am not sure if this is a problem with Windows too. Also During cutscenes, some of the shadows are glitched and flicker.
title: Sunset Overdrive

notes:
Cutscenes have glitchy shaders. Menu has frozen the game once or twice. Game eventually crashed after an hour of usage (could be a port problem). Generally music  , graphics and gameplay all work fine and aren't interrupted. Performance is good and at times feels like a native game.

Get's a silver as it requires tweaking Esync, and can crash early or late into the game. However, frequent autosaves make that bearable.
title: Sunset Overdrive

notes: If Esync is not disabled the game will crash at the title screen. The first 15 minutes or so stutter heavily due to shader compilation but after that the game runs flawlessly. The only problem with it, and the reason that I'm calling it silver, is that it has a tendency to randomly crash. It could happen after 30 minutes or 3 hours. The autosave system is pretty robust so I've never lost more than a minute or two of progress because of it, but it is something to keep in mind.
title: Sunset Overdrive

notes: Crashes during intro cinematic regardless of Proton version, even with ESYNC disabled.
title: Sunset Overdrive

notes: Much improved as of Proton 3.16-6. Still requires disabling esync to avoid crashes, though the game is far more stable using this tweak than it was before. Graphics errors during cut scenes and game play have also been drastically reduced. Heavy shader cache stutter when starting the game that goes away over time.
title: Sunset Overdrive

notes: Runs nicely but has minor graphical artifacts and often crashes, sometimes minutes into the game, sometimes over an hour playing. Doesn't work properly with Linux Steam Integration, use the bundled runtime instead of the native runtime.
title: Sunset Overdrive

notes: With esync disabled it crashes in the intro sequence.
title: Sunset Overdrive
